checks.detail: All required files present.
solution/solve.sh, oracle.yaml, and job.yaml all present.
All required fields present and within limits.
git and tmux are installed via apt-get.
tests/ contains extra files beyond test.sh.
reasons: compute_reward.py and run_all.py should live in environment/tests/ and be COPYed into the Docker image

checks.detail: Stray ANTI_CHEAT.md at task root.
reasons: ANTI_CHEAT.md is not part of the Harbor task spec and no .gitignore exists to exclude it

notes: Move tests/compute_reward.py and tests/run_all.py into environment/tests/ and COPY them into /tests/ in the Dockerfile, leaving only test.sh at tests/.
Remove or relocate ANTI_CHEAT.md from the task root, or add a .gitignore to exclude developer notes.

checks.detail: task.toml [agent] user = "agent" is set.
All required isolation primitives present in Dockerfile.
reasons: present: useradd agent (default uid 1000)
present: chown -R agent:agent /app
present: chown agent:agent /solution
present: mkdir /tests && chmod 700 /tests (re-asserted with go-rwx strip, fixtures pruned)
present: strace installed (apt-get) for runtime tripwires
present: git + tmux installed (Harbor hard-rule tooling)
present: gcc/build-essential available for verifier-side checks

checks.detail: test.sh locks /logs/verifier first, runs agent build under su+strace+timeout, scores outside strace, and always exits 0.
reasons: present: chmod 700 /logs/verifier BEFORE rm -rf wipe
present: re-asserts chmod 700 /tests post-upload
present: pkill -u agent + survival check before scoring
present: env sanitization (LD_PRELOAD, PYTHONPATH, CARGO_*, RUSTFLAGS)
present: cargo build runs via `su agent` under `strace -f` with `timeout 3000` and `env -i`
present: strace tripwire greps for openat write on reward.{txt,json}
present: agent binary execution delegated to run_all.py (Phase 1) with cheat.txt tripwire
present: Phase 2 scoring is pure file diff + compute_reward.py — no agent code imported
present: emit_fail fallback writes reward via compute_reward.py on every error path
present: exit 0 on every termination path; no `set -euo pipefail`
present: no chown root:root on /logs directories

checks.detail: runs_agent_code — verifier compiles and executes the agent's Rust binary.
reasons: agent crate is built with `cargo build --release` (build.rs executes)
compiled $RLS binary is executed in Phase 1 (run_all.py) to capture outputs
strace + su agent + env -i required and present

checks.detail: No external Python or Cargo dependencies are declared in the task; Rust toolchain is installed via rustup and no Cargo.toml/requirements files exist.
No Python packages are installed via pip in the Dockerfile; only apt packages and rustup toolchain are used.
task.toml does not set docker_image; expected during development but must be set before final delivery.
reasons: docker_image not set in [environment]

notes: Set docker_image in task.toml [environment] before final delivery to pin the prebuilt image.

checks.detail: Deliverable, invocation, flags, env vars, and output format are clearly specified.
Explicit 2-hour (7200s) budget with iteration guidance and a final-build reminder.
Largely spec-like with tables rather than conversational handoff, but readable.
reasons: Heavy use of reference-style tables instead of colleague prose

checks.detail: 128 lines exceeds the 100-line guideline; flag tables could move to a workspace reference doc.
reasons: 128 lines

checks.detail: Contains an 'Evaluation' section describing comparison against GNU ls with normalization, which leaks verifier behavior.
reasons: '## Evaluation' header
'compared against GNU ls ... it must match exactly'
'trailing-whitespace normalization on each line'
'Each test has a per-case timeout (10 seconds)' references tests

checks.detail: Names Rust, allowed crates (libc/nix/clap), forbids uutils, and points to /app/workspace/examples and /app/fixtures.

notes: Rename/rework the 'Evaluation' section to avoid leaking verifier mechanics (drop 'compared against', 'normalization', 'per-case timeout'); reframe as expected behavior the user cares about.
Consider moving the flag tables to a reference doc under /app/workspace/ to bring the instruction under 100 lines and adopt a more conversational tone.

checks.detail: Missing /app/workspace/Cargo.toml triggers emit_fail not_a_rust_project with score 0.0.
Reward file lockdown, /tests root-only, env -i scrubbing, encrypted oracle, sha256 identity check, and strace tripwires close all validated attack vectors.
Both cargo build (build.rs) and the rls binary execute via su agent -s /bin/bash -c under strace -f.
strace -f wraps both the build and every per-case agent invocation; logs scanned for reward openat and banned execve before scoring.
compute_reward.py runs python3 -ISs, takes only argv + a root-written subscores file, never imports anything from /app.
All failure paths route through emit_fail (writes 0.0 atomically) and exit 0; build/oracle/agent invocations are wrapped in timeouts.
Each test requires byte-exact diff against GNU ls output; broken/missing binary yields empty .actual file and 0 passes.
Reward measures byte-identical stdout vs GNU ls — any Rust implementation that reproduces the output scores, not tied to a specific algorithm.
Three equally-weighted tiers aggregated via weighted sum (≈arithmetic mean); tier1_basic is much easier than tier2_long_format, letting an agent harvest ~33% by acing only tier1.
reasons: Weighted sum across tiers permits gaming the easiest dimension
tier1_basic (no-flag/-1/-a/-A listing) is substantially easier than tier2 (perms, owner, sizes, dates, symlinks columns) and tier3 (version sort, multi-key sort)

checks.detail: Trivial outputs (empty, constant, copy-of-input) cannot match GNU ls's exact long-format/sorted output, and byte-diff has zero partial credit floor.
compute_reward.py reads only a verifier-written subscores.txt and never imports or touches /app, so it can be re-run independently.
Both emit_fail and emit_score write reward.json with score (float), subscores (list, [] on failure), and additional_data (dict) atomically, plus reward.txt.

notes: Replace the weighted-sum aggregation across tiers with a power mean (e.g. p=-1 harmonic-style) or multiplicative aggregation so an agent that aces only tier1_basic cannot collect ~33% of the score while ignoring long-format and sorting.
Alternatively, gate any tier credit on a minimum cross-tier pass threshold (e.g. require >0 passes in every tier before crediting any) to prevent single-dimension harvesting.

checks.detail: Scored tiers (1 basic, 2 long, 3 sorting), flag set, env vars (LC_ALL/TZ/COLUMNS/TERM), and trailing-whitespace-per-line normalization all match between instruction.md and run_all.py/compute_reward.py.
All required flags map to standard GNU ls semantics; version sort, recent-vs-old date switch, and LC_ALL=C byte-order sorting are explicitly defined in instruction.md.

Rust toolchain is installed system-wide and the agent user can build, but instruction.md names /app/workspace/examples/expected/t2_basic_long.txt while build_tests.py copies only the first 6 alphabetically sorted t*_basic_*.expected files — which are all tier1 (t1_basic_*), so the named t2 file never lands in the workspace.
reasons: instruction.md line 115 references examples/expected/t2_basic_long.txt; create_examples() in build_tests.py copies only the first 6 sorted matches, which excludes any t2_/t3_ basic outputs

checks.detail: solution/solve.sh exists, writes the HARBOR_ORACLE_FLAG marker, and decrypts the baked oracle ls into /app/workspace/rls.
All test cases are structural permutations of the documented flags on documented fixture trees; no scoring dimension beyond byte-equality with GNU ls.

notes: Fix the example diff command in instruction.md: either widen the create_examples() glob in environment/build_tests.py to also cover t2_basic_long.expected (e.g. explicitly include one per tier) or change the instruction example to a filename that is actually copied (e.g. t1_basic_no_flags.txt).

checks.detail: Slim base and apt cache cleaned, but `bc` is installed and never used anywhere in the verifier or build pipeline.
reasons: unused: bc package (scoring moved to compute_reward.py — no shell arithmetic remains)

checks.detail: Only solve.sh present; decrypts the baked oracle and writes the oracle marker — clean and minimal.
Core hardening is necessary and well-justified, but two minor items are stale given the actual /tests contents and image.
reasons: useful: chmod 700 + wipe /logs/verifier (mandatory)
useful: chmod 700 /tests re-assertion after Harbor's verify-phase upload resets perms
useful: env scrub + pkill agent + env -i around build (build.rs is agent code)
useful: strace tripwires on both build and run phases (build.rs and binary are agent-controlled)
useful: openssl ELF check on decrypted oracle (catches ciphertext tampering)
useful: binary sha256 == oracle check (blocks the obvious copy-the-oracle cheat)
redundant: `find /tests -type f -name '*.sh' -exec chmod +x` — only test.sh lives under /tests; no helper .sh scripts to restore
redundant: `grep -rq ls_backup /app/workspace/` static check — no ls_backup binary is ever created by the Dockerfile (system ls/dir/vdir are replaced in place, not backed up)

checks.detail: Repo is ~896K total with no individual file over 30MB; no LFS candidates.
environment/workspace/ contains only examples/README.md; fixtures are generated at image build time, no stale configs or dead code.

notes: Remove `bc` from the apt install list and update the inline comment in environment/Dockerfile.
Drop the stale `find /tests ... chmod +x` line and the `ls_backup` static grep from tests/test.sh.

instruction_quality.task_summary: Build a Rust binary at /app/workspace/rls that clones GNU coreutils ls. Must support listing/filtering, long-format, and sorting flags with byte-identical output to GNU ls.

measures: chmod 700 /logs/verifier then rm -rf at test.sh start
chmod 700 /tests re-asserted post-upload, go-rwx stripped on all non-fixture files
Agent binary copied to root-owned $BIN_DIR/rls 0755 before execution
Agent user runs build.rs and the rls binary via su agent -s /bin/bash -c
Oracle decrypt, fixture stage, and scoring run as root outside agent reach
env -i wipes verifier vars (ORACLE/FIXTURES/TESTS_DEFS_DIR) for both build and runtime
test.sh unsets LD_PRELOAD/LD_LIBRARY_PATH/PYTHON*/BASH_ENV/CARGO*/RUSTFLAGS
GNU ls AES-256-CBC encrypted in image (key only in root-only test.sh)
Decrypted oracle written to root-only WORK_DIR, ELF validated
sha256 equality check rejects byte-identical agent binary
strace -f on cargo build catches build.rs reward writes
Per-case strace catches openat of reward.{txt,json} with write flags
Per-case strace catches execve of ls/dir/vdir/oracle basename
Pre-build rm of /app/workspace/target/release/<binname> and legacy rls
Binary name resolved from Cargo.toml [[bin]].name / package.name (no glob)
ELF + executable check on the copied binary
greps for /oracle/, .test_data, oracle.enc, decryption key, reward paths, uutils/coreutils, ls_backup, /usr/bin/dir|vdir
pkill -9 -u agent with retry before scoring; refuses to score if agent procs survive
compute_reward.py invoked with python3 -ISs, never imports /app
Atomic write via tmpfile + os.replace for reward.json/reward.txt
No set -euo pipefail (avoids early abort leaving planted reward)
All failure branches call emit_fail then exit 0
timeout wrappers on cargo build (3000s) and each agent run

acknowledged_vectors.vector: /logs/agent is 777 — agent can tamper with its own trajectory logs
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Documented Harbor limitation; does not affect reward computation

acknowledged_vectors.vector: build.rs executes as agent user during cargo build
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Necessary for Rust builds; covered by strace tripwire on reward writes and by env -i scrubbing of verifier paths

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Agent owns /app/workspace and can plant files during the agent phase
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Agent binary is copied to root-owned $BIN_DIR before execution; static greps catch known cheat strings; oracle/test artifacts live in root-only /tests

reward_design.formula: score = sum_over_tiers( (passed_in_tier / total_in_tier) * weight_tier ), weights = {tier1_basic: 1/3, tier2_long_format: 1/3, tier3_sorting: 1/3}
reward_design.formula_explanation: Per-tier pass-rate weighted sum across the three in-scope tiers, where each test is a binary byte-exact diff of agent stdout against GNU ls.

components.name: tier1_basic
components.metric: fraction of basic-listing tests with byte-exact stdout match vs GNU ls

components.name: tier2_long_format
components.metric: fraction of long-format tests with byte-exact stdout match vs GNU ls

components.name: tier3_sorting
components.metric: fraction of sorting tests with byte-exact stdout match vs GNU ls

components.name: anti_cheat_gate
components.metric: static workspace grep + strace tripwire (reward writes, execve of ls/dir/vdir/oracle) + build-from-source enforcement + oracle-hash inequality

dimension_analysis.name: tier1_basic
dimension_analysis.notes: 30 tests; only requires correct entry enumeration, -1/-a/-A semantics, ASCII byte-order sort — small surface, easiest tier to clean-sweep.

dimension_analysis.name: tier2_long_format
dimension_analysis.notes: ~46 tests covering -l/-g/-o/-G/-n/-i/-s/--author/-L with exact column alignment, perms+setuid/setgid/sticky, owner/group lookups, date formatting (recent vs old), symlink target rendering — broadest and most failure-prone tier.

dimension_analysis.name: tier3_sorting
dimension_analysis.notes: ~50 tests across -S/-t/-r/-U/-v/-X/-f/--sort=WORD/--group-directories-first plus combos with -l; version sort and stable tie-breaking are the main traps.

redundant_items.item: `bc` package in apt-get install (line 15) and the matching comment 'verifier helpers (openssl/bc/file)' on line 3
redundant_items.why: Scoring is fully in Python (compute_reward.py); no shell uses bc — drop the package and update the comment.
Only test.sh exists under /tests; Harbor already chmods test.sh executable on upload. No other .sh scripts to repair.
The Dockerfile replaces /usr/bin/{ls,dir,vdir} in place — no ls_backup binary exists anywhere in the image, so this grep can never match a real cheat.
